reviews.title: Great budget keycaps
reviews.text: Please don’t mind my cat lol she’s modeling the keyboard. Really great budget keycaps, super pretty color. I use aqua king switches and the sound with the keycaps is pretty good. Really like and enjoy them. P.S. the yellow keycaps ARE NOT part of the set, they are from another keycap set. I thought it would compliment the purple nicely :)

reviews.title: Nice keycaps, but holy crap they're snug
reviews.text:
These keycaps are probably best suited for soldered switches. If you're on a hot swap board, get ready to separate every key from the cap as you'll be pulling the entire assembly out EVERY TIME.

Other than my tolerance/keycap ripping issues these are solid and have a good sound indicative of a good pbt material.

reviews.title: Great keycap set, just wish they we're backlight compatible.
reviews.text: That's my fault, I always assume keycaps are backlight compatible but they feel awesome, love the satin touch, they look great, and the legend is super bright and clear. If you're not specifically looking for a keycap set to use with a backlit keyboard, this one looks great.

reviews.title: So far so good! Note to buyers with costar stabilizers
reviews.text: Nice quality for the price, easy to install, keys came in a plastic tray so you can store your old keycaps in order if needed. Note to those with costar stabilizers, the key caps do not come with MX Keycap Stabilizer Inserts... so if your old keys are like mine and the old ones are apparently glued in, you'll need these to finish the keyboard. You can find them to 3d print pretty easily.

reviews.title: Printed partially
reviews.text: The letters are not oriented fully. Bothers me because I can lines through the letters.

reviews.title: I type so well on these
reviews.text: I didn't think a different key profile would do much for me, but my accuracy immediately went up with the XDA profile. These were a great price among the similar sets I found and were an affordable way to dive into my first customization. I wanted some kinda retro keys and I just love em!

reviews.title: !!!!!Great PCB!!!!!!!!
reviews.text:
Used this for my Switch Couture Electrotype60 amazing PCB and pretty easy to solder, especially for an amateur solderer like me, definitely will be buying another one when I have another case to build. Fit perfectly, No problems soldering, and definitely came out perfect out of the box, though I don't know how to brighten up the LED nor change the modes, I'm completely fine with the RGB cycle. I'm Typing on my new keyboard as we speak and it works perfectly, no delays, no nothing, just perfect!!!!

(I can admit I'm kinda too last to do VIA so I'm just working with it lol)

title: SNAP 75% Mechanical Keyboard Kit (White)
description: A stylish, 75% split The SNAP is a 75% split mechanical keyboard kit with support for 2 rotary encoders, 2 OLED displays, speaker, and RGB underglow. We took all the best parts of the NIBBLE and added some of the most popular requests: true split shift/space/backspace. The SNAP is configurable, programmable, and easily assembled. 100% through hole design The SNAP uses a Pro Micro MCU instead of a discrete micro-controller chip. It offers the fun and aesthetic of a kit without having to worry about soldering the difficult bits like USB connectors. All required parts diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder. underglow LEDs are surface-mount Multiple Layout Options Use the SNAP in split mode, or snap it whoa, get it?) together for a more traditional layout. Strong internal magnets hold the two sides together and magical spring-loaded pogo pin contacts mean there's no need for a TRRS cable when the two sides are attached. The SNAP supports many configurations and layouts: Split space/backspace, split shift, ISO enter, and more. Since it runs QMK firmware and uses VIA, it's easy to change keymaps, program macros, and customize functionality. RGB underglow ready 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. Included Top & bottom PCB Acrylic supports M2 standoffs & screws Diodes, ICs, decoupling caps, reset switch RGB underglow, speaker, magnets, and pogo pins Rotary encoder & color-matched knob TRRS cable and mini bumpons Not included 2x Bit-Cs or Pro Micro Compatible MCU MX-compatible switches & keycaps PCB-mount stabilizers NOTE: This is a kit which requires assembly and soldering! Bit-Cs, switches, keycaps, and other accessories are sold separately
features: The SNAP is a high-quality split 75% keyboard kit with tons of room for customization
Use the SNAP together, or separate! It snaps together for a traditional 75% layout or pulls apart for use as a split board
Built around a Pro Micro, rather than a discrete microcontroller. This saves assembly time, and also reduces the build difficulty
All required parts (diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder

RGB underglow ready: 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. RGB underglow is supported natively in QMK, allowing for easy programming and configuration

reviews.title: Not for the faint of heart
reviews.text:
This kit does take a lot of work so I would not recommend it as a first build, or for someone looking for a quick build.

It is also a fairly unique one of a kind kit. I don't know of other split 75% keyboards with pro micro support that also have great documentation, support and community. If you are willing to put in the time you will be rewarded with a unique keyboard, and the kit really is fun to put together for experienced builders.

I would knock down half star as I encountered some issues with the fitting of the acrylic plate, specifically in these areas:
1. acrylic plate fitting at the keyboard split edge, had to use an exacto knife
2. acrylic plate fitting with bottom screws of the PCB mount stabilizers, had to make the holes wider
3. snap magnets did not fit, gave up trying to fit these
4. components pin through the solder joints interfered with acrylic, this is expected and is easily fixable by trimming them down.

Apart from that everything worked as expected. Firmware worked on first try and I was easily able to get it working with VIA.

reviews.title: Nice mechanical keyboard for the money
reviews.text: I have a number of mechanical keyboards that are mostly used for business purposes. My primary motivator for buying this keyboard was to try out the GamaKay Phoenix switches, but I also like the look and size of the keyboard. The price is also really good, in my opinion. Right now this keyboard is the one I reach for the most. I really like the quiet thump of the switches and they feel nice to use, but to be clear - they aren't actually silent. I've had a few annoyances with this keyboard occasionally not "waking up". The lights will respond, but typing doesn't show up. It's an easy fix. I just switch the input type over to BT and then back to USB since I've been using it wired. It immediately starts responding again. This isn't a consistent issue, but is a small thing that I wish didn't happen.

reviews.title: Support is impressive
reviews.text: Great keyboard back up by great service.

reviews.title: Easy to pair; good customizability
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:4822e6b7aa201be2a20c1d13ef6c2077]] Pros:

- Compact. Good mechanical alternative to Magic keyboard if size is an important factor for you.
- Customizable. Hotswappable switches and caps give you a good platform for changes later.

Cons:

- Stock caps are very plasticky and not the best I've used. Print quality and shape are good, but the material makes them feel hollow and gives an odd echo to each keypress. Will be replacing.
- No risers. The flat angle of the keyboard is not comfortable. Needs some sort of elevation in the back.

Meh:

-Stock switches are not the best I've used, not the worst. The yellow gateron's are among my favorite switches. They have a great overall feel. Some keys, particularly spacebar, have some "springy" sound to them and need lubrication or cushioning. Fortunately, the switches are swappable, and this can be adjusted if you're down for further investment and/or a project.
- Bluetooth connects well, but is sometimes laggy on input. Not a huge deal for regular typing, but I wouldn't game on it.
- LED's are always "moving." There's no way to set it to one color and leave it, which is my preferred color set.

Bottom line:

This keyboard is a decent platform for customization and you can MAKE this keyboard into exactly what you want, but I think it's overpriced if that's what you're getting it for.

reviews.title: My favorite keyboard!
reviews.text: I own quite a few of these multi-mode mechanical keyboards, and this is my favorite one by far. The build quality is phenomenal. PBT plastic keycaps with an XDA profile, dual flip out feet, the small layout WITH dedicated arrow keys, and even RGB backlighting. The keyboard uses a USB-C connection for wired mode. You also get a 2.4ghz dongle for wireless connectivity if you don't have Bluetooth. If you do, then you can connect and swap between 3 different Bluetooth devices. The ON/OFF switch and the connection mode switch are on the left side of the keyboard as opposed to the bottom. The RGB backlighting shines around the keycaps but not through them as PBT does not play well with allowing backlighting through a key as it is firmer and heavier than ABS. Still, with the white case cover the lighting looks great. You can also turn off backlighting to extend the battery life of the built-in battery. If you download the driver software, you can easily change the backlighting options without having to worry about remembering too many key combinations. This keyboard looks good and feels better. I got the option with the silver speed switches, and it is a dream with this keyboard. The speed of the actuation plus the feel of the solid PBT makes this keyboard the most comfortable that I own. This keyboard is now my daily driver, even above my HHKB. I highly recommend you try this keyboard. If you want a reduced layout with solid construction and fast response, you cannot get better than this anywhere.

reviews.title: Nice keys, font size a little horsey
reviews.text:
Note: I chose the Grey/Beige set. This set (YMDK 96 84 ANSI ISO Key) went on just fine, and are a great frugal find.

I bought these for an relatively inexpensive TKL mechanical wireless keyboard (RK87G) because the OEM keycaps were set in a truly fugly and illegible typeface.

These YMDKs feel good, and have a slightly deeper "thunk" when they bottom out. The two-character keys are typeset vertically, as Underwood and Remington intended. Although the font size on most of the keys is a big, they are fine and give the keyboard a bit of a homemade retro look.

NOTE Specific to the RK87G: This YMDK set does not have a ScrlLk key. The right Shift and right CTRL on this set are a little too small, so I just left the old ones on. I also left the OEM ScrlLk, Page Up, and arrow keys on because they have symbols for controlling the RGB LEDs. If you use the RKs function keys for app and control shortcuts, you may want to leave those on as well.

May 30, 2021
I've had these for a little over six months now. Still a great value set the money. I installed them on a keyboard I use for hours on end nearly every day. The printing on the beige keys hasn't faded, but I've noticed that the grey keys with white printing are beginning to fade. I'm not taking any stars off because before I purchased these I had already read other reviewers who had the same experience.

reviews.title: Good for the money

reviews.title: Rough surface, washed out colors
reviews.text:
These caps seems well made enough, with a rough texture that provides ample grip without having a sandpaper effect on your fingers. The colors are a fair bit less saturated than the images make them look. Slightly annoying but they're still quite nice.

Do note that this set does not come with a long shift or scroll lock cap. This isn't an issue as they'll be going on a 65% for me but if you're looking to put them on a full size board you may be disappointed.

reviews.title: Great pre-solder build. Comes with two cherry mx blacks and the Bluetooth is tricky.
reviews.text: Very good build quality overall. It came with two cherry mx black switches for the space bar and the right control keys which is a huge bonus. I purchased the Bluetooth white pcb which is very well built but the Bluetooth is very tricky. I have not been able to set it up yet even when I try to do the “fn+zxc” keys to setup for pairing it doesn’t work. Other than that it was a great purchase for the price and I will eventually figure out the Bluetooth.

reviews.title: Awesome first kit
reviews.text: First I want to state why I removed 1 star. I was missing 2 tiny screws that are easy to replace. However, the seller was very quick to reply and gave me all the details I needed to replace it. They even offered to fix the issue for me. But I am ok doing it myself. This is my first board build and I used Kahil Box Navy blues. The board feels amazing and well put together. Great first kit to get started. Can't wait to build my next board and get some more parts to form YMDK. I would choose the faster shipping if you are like me and hate waiting. I did get the board quicker than the projected date.

this is a really great for the money. fit and finish is really pretty good, not great, but real good. the overall design is great, and the implementation is good. the clip in stabilizers are "lacking" but thats not a serious knock on the kit.

It is a good kit for the money for sure.

title: G.SKILL USB KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard, Cherry MX Red, ABS Dual Injection Keycap, (Black)
description: The G.SKILL KM360 tenkeyless mechanical keyboard is a compact 87-key layout keyboard designed for durability. Featuring long-lasting Cherry MX mechanical keyswitches, wear-proof ABS double injection keycaps, and a solid aluminum top-plate, the KM360 mechanical keyboard is the perfect daily mechanical keyboard. With a detachable USB Type-C cable, it's easy to replace a worn cable by simply swapping it out with another USB Type-C cable of your choice.
features: CHERRY MX - Most popular mechanical keyswitch for their high standards of quality, reliability, and durability; rated for over 50 million keypresses.
COMPACT & PORTABLE - Designed with a detachable USB Type-C cable and a tenkeyless layout for more desk space and great portability.
WEAR-PROOF KEYS - Using ABS dual-injection keycaps mean the keys won't wear out like printed or laser etched keycaps.
HIGH DURABILITY - Constructed with a solid aluminum top plate and floating keyswitches for easy cleaning.
MODERN DESIGN - Featuring ergonomic keys, white LED backlight, and n-key rollover for comfort and ease of use.

reviews.title: Clicky clack who’s at the rack with the jackets? Me!
reviews.text:
I had been eyeing a mechanical keyboard for years, hesitant to buy one because A) I already had a perfectly fine working keyboard and B) Reviews I had read made it sound like they were so noisy you needed to wear ear protection while using one. During a recent move I left my previously mentioned perfectly fine working keyboard outside for an extended period of time, uncovered in the rain and sun and any other elements that rolled though Southern California for those few months. When I finally returned to the keyboard, I found that it had become yet another casualty of the pandemic. Finally I had good cause for a new keyboard, and my research led me to try this one. And like the famous tattoo says, I have no ragrets!

I plugged it in and immediately it lit up and off my little fingies went, zipping around the keyboard with the grace and elegance of a gold medal winning gymnast at the 2021 Tokyo Olympics. Every click and clack was like music to my ears, each sentence I typed sounding like a symphony composed by Mozart himself! I now look forward to typing my weekly emails to mother, catching her up with her favorite son’s (sorry Richard, but you know it’s true!) comings and goings!
I’ve only had this keyboard for about a week, so time will tell if it will hold up to the rigorous poundings that accompany each email I compose, but as of now I would not hesitate to recommend this keyboard to all my many (ok, zero) friends! Now I can’t wait to get off from my day job as a clerk typist and go home and get some typing in!

reviews.title: Great Initial Impression
reviews.text:
My first foray into mechanical KB and cherry switches. I was familiar with GSkill as they are a trusted brand in performance PC DDR4 RAM. I liked the price point and the size was perfect. Smaller than a full size but still having arrow keys and delete, etc off to the side.
I’m no KB expert, but it appears to be of good quality and finish. Nice adjustable LED backlighting. It has a sleeved nylon cable that’s white and matches the board nicely. Typing on it will take some getting used to, but I’m liking it so far and will update later if there’s any issues.

reviews.title: Amazing keyboard EDIT:
reviews.text: Amazing keyboard.  I'm typing on my new G.SKILL KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard and YES it amazing!  Seriously if you have the money by this product.  Totally worth every penny! It's been just over a year since I got this mechanical keyboard & the USB type C is coming loose, I have to move the cord back N forth so it will connect again. Not sure if it can be fixed or not. (EDIT) I got this in October of 2019 & the small side of the USB Type-C female side is breaking off, causing the keyboard to flicker on and off. Other than that it's awesome keyboard

reviews.title: Great keyboard from a regular joe
reviews.text:
This is my first keyboard and saw great review. I’m not a big gamer or anything but recently started playing a little on a gaming laptop and thought why not. I thought the keyboard on the laptop were fine but damn these are better. I started using them for work and everything and love this key board now. So if your on the fence just do it.

10/10

reviews.title: Almost perfect
reviews.text: Exactly what I was looking for in an TKL board. The only thing that’s below standard are the kick stands. They don’t provide much resistance, so when you just nudge the keyboard forward the kickstands collapse - not sure if they need to be more sturdy or have a more grippy rubber foot but a rather disappointing feature to an otherwise perfect set up.

title: NACODEX 132 Keys Gradient Blue PBT Keycaps Set, Cherry Profile Lake Blue Summer Keycap for 61/87/ 104/108 Mechanical Keyboard, Compatible with Cherry/Gateron/Otemu/Kailh Switch
features: 【Blue Gradient Keycaps】The keycap set gradually changes from blue to white, inspired by "East Dawning". At dawn, when the sun in the east exceeds the horizon, the blue sky gradually reveals white dawn (it's dawn), which means opening a new day. A day that brings hope and confidence to your life.

【132 Custom Keycaps Set】Blue Gradient Keycaps besides the basic full keycaps, this set also contains extra replacement keycaps. It provides more fun to match keyboards with different layouts, no matter your keyboard is 60%, 65%, 75%, TKL, 100% or 108 key mechanical keyboard, adding color to your desktop, it is the perfect keycap replacement on the market.

【Comfortable Cherry Profile】Adopt the popular cherry profile on the market, the height is 2mm lower than the OEM keycap, suitable for those who like ergonomic comfortable height typing experience, if you are looking for something that can improve your gaming experience, don’t spend milliseconds come find your next key, just set this Cherry Profile PBT keycap for efficient and comfortable keys.

【PBT Keycaps with High-Quality Material】Pbt double shot keycaps adopts two-color injection molding technology and PBT material. Compared with ordinary ABS keycaps, it feels more delicate and comfortable. It is very dry and not easy to oil, not like ABS. Simplified closed characters, high hardness, never fade.

【Strong compatibility with MX Switches】132 blue keycap set in the exquisite box, it only has keycaps, not including the keyboard, it is compatible with MX structure switches, such as Cherry, Gateron, Kailh, TTC, etc., you can easily and quickly DIY your mechanical keyboard becomes the ideal and best choice when you are looking for a replacement set.

reviews.title: Just what I was looking for
reviews.text: Great quality, love that it comes with multiple different colors of the same key so that you can customize it. So happy with this purchase!!!

reviews.title: Good feeling, good overall design
reviews.text: Was able to use these keys on my gaming keyboard.  It provided a good feeling as I press on the keys.  It’s Very responsive.  The only downside to this is that the rgb lights on my keyboard does not pass through the keys…not the end of the world but overall a good set of keys.  The picture on the website is deceiving as it’s just keys to replace an existing keyboard.  It does not come with the keyboard itself.

reviews.title: You Like Red, White & Blue So Make Your Keyboard Reflect That!
reviews.text: We started doing this with our “high-end” computer buyers. We take a premium – Cherry or similar – keyboard and remove all or most of the key tops replacing them with the various colors available making a color complementary computer input device. It makes for a more interesting keyboard, which obviously is the main interface with your computer, and it gives our customers something so unique as to be one-of-a-kind. All the keyboards start with premium red, blue, brown, or black key actuators which, without exception, are the best available. That together with our customer’s keytops “artwork” gives a person something exclusive as well as offering superior functionality. The keytops come right off use the small plastic tool most of the keytop provides include with their kits and doing a whole keyboard only takes about 20 minutes. One thing you do have to watch for is the keytop height. Many times, they will make certain keys a little higher. This poses no issue unless you stick a high key in the middle of the number/letters, then this may impede faster typists. Remember each key actuator that is attached to the base of the keyboard is programmed to send a certain signal to the computer thus causing your monitor to display that letter/number or take that action. For example, if you put the “M” keytop where the “T” was that will still send a “T” to your computer and monitor. I would recommend removing and replacing 2 or 3 at a time or having a like keyboard to refer to, so the possibility for confusion is limited. All keyboards don’t have the exact layout especially in the extended section and number pad. Also, some of the higher end gaming style keyboards have extra programmable keys that are specified through the keyboard manufacturer’s software. Some of the keytop set will provide blank keys to use in these instances. Buy a couple sets and have fun making your own custom keyboard.

reviews.title: Great color for white keyboard, good quality
reviews.text: The NACODEX 129 Keys PBT Keycaps Set is a high-quality and visually appealing upgrade for any mechanical keyboard. The keycaps are made of durable PBT material and feature a JSA profile and double shot design. The pink and white color combination gives a unique touch to the keyboard, making it a great choice for those who want to personalize their setup. The keycaps are compatible with Cherry, Gateron, Otemu, and Kailh switches, making them a versatile choice for many different types of mechanical keyboards. Overall, this keycap set is a great investment for anyone looking to improve the look and feel of their keyboard.

reviews.title: These keycaps feel and look awesome (for the price)
reviews.text:
I bought these keycaps for a cheap mechanical keyboard I had lying around. I had put some cheap keycaps of a similar price but a different brand on them earlier this year. Didn't like those keycaps at all.

These keycaps have a more pleasant texture, not smooth but not quite as grainy. I love love love the greyscale color scheme, and the blank keycaps are really aesthetically pleasing. Obviously, if you can't type without looking at your keyboard you should purchase the keycaps that come with all the lettering, it probably won't take away much from your experience.

One small note, I would suggest caution when using the included keycap puller. It scratched the side of my spacebar and left a little black line. Unfortunately, it is on the side of the keyboard that faces me, so now every time I sit down to type I will see the little black scratch. So be careful when pulling these off. Otherwise, I love these colors and will definitely consider buying other products from this brand based on this level of quality for price.

I forgot to mention. I loved the packaging on these keycaps!! The other brand I bought from had the keycaps all together in a ziplock bag. This brand had the keycaps arranged in order in sleeves and it was so much better for putting the keycaps on! ok that's all.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text: Son loves them

reviews.title: these keycaps suck
reviews.text: these keycaps are uneven dont waste your money they are ugly in person

title: DROP + Invyr Holy Panda Mechanical Keyboard Switches - Plate Mounted Tactile Switches, Cherry MX Style, 67g Spring, by Kailh (70 PCS)
features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

reviews.title: High Quality Caps - Adore the Theme
reviews.text: As a typist, these caps have a great feel to them. They are thick without being heavy. No wobble. Improve the sound of the board and switch. I purchased the Smoke Cloud theme, and the images are adorable for a weather enthusiast like me. This set also had plenty of extra keys to complete the 96% keyboards, which not a lot of sets include. I was leary of paying $40 for a set without trying them first, but I don't regret it - great purchase - fast shipping!

reviews.title: Looks great, sounds like quality
reviews.text: I'm a bit of a keycap junkie.  I love being able to swap keys out to give my keyboard a different look.  If you're looking at this, you likely already like the look and I think they look better in person.  The keycaps are easy to install and provide good sound.  No hollow cheapness to these!

reviews.title: Very thick keycaps
reviews.text:
What I like
I really like the quality of the prints on these keycaps.
Also the thickness of these keycaps are even past some doubleshot ones I have.

What I find questionable.
The character keys looked like they were a light green color, but they are bright white. I ended up liking them anyway, just was a bit surprised.

Overall they are a great set of keycaps, going to keep using them for sure.

reviews.title: Great Authentic Cherry Switches

title: Azio Retro Classic Bluetooth (Elwood) - Wireless/USB Wired Walnut Wood Vintage Backlit Mechanical Keyboard for PC/Mac, Walnut Wood (MK-RETRO-W-01B-US) & RM-RCM-L-03 Retro Classic Mouse (Artisan)
description: Azio Retro Classic Bluetooth (Elwood) - Luxury Vintage Backlit Mechanical Keyboard, Metallic Brown/Grey (MK-RETRO-W-01B-US)Inspired by vintage typewriters and crafted with ultramodern features, the Azio Retro Classic is the perfect amalgam of past, present and future. The retro classic is a backlit mechanical keyboard with round keycaps that evokes nostalgia of yesteryear complemented with a durable aluminum frame and a sumptuous genuine leather or walnut surface.Azio RM-RCM-L-03 Retro Classic Mouse (Artisan)The retro classic mouse (RCM) stays true to the minimalist philosophy in order to accentuate its ambidextrous design. With its modern features and ability to work on virtually any surface, The RCM is an essential companion for your retro classic & compact keyboard.
features: Product 1: A classic reborn: design originated by vintage typewriters and crafted with ultramodern features, the Azio Retro Classic is the perfect amalgam of past, present, and future.
Product 1: Genuine & luxurious: Genuine leather or wood top plate. Leather reflects taste, craftsmanship, and exclusiveness while emitting a unique charm that inspires.
Product 1: Tactile. Clicky. Backlit: the backlit mechanical keys are tuned to be tactile and "clicky", reminiscent of vintage typewriters.
Product 1: Forged. Polished. Plated: The keyboard frame is forged with aluminum alloy and plated into a beautiful chrome/Satin finish to accompany the leather top plate.
Product 2: GENUINE LEATHER SURFACE - Utilizing top-grain chrome-tanned leather to provide a pleasant touch, each leather piece is precision-cut, molded in, and hand-trimmed to perfection.

Product 2: EXQUISITE CRAFTMANSHIP - Immaculate attention to every detail - Genuine leather top cover, textured aluminum wheel and alloy frame with precision bolts. The scroll wheel design starts with a high-grade aluminum rod that is knurled into a diamond-shaped pattern and completed with chamfered edges on a precision CNC lathe. It is then anodized to enhance aesthetics and durability.
Product 2: TRACKS Virtually ANYWHERE - The PixArt PAW3805 sensor is designed to track flawlessly on virtually any surface, including glass and other high-gloss materials.

Product 2: DUAL USB-RF & BLUETOOTH - Interfaces Connect wirelessly with Bluetooth or RF via USB receiver. Works on Bluetooth enabled PC's natively but also includes a USB RF receiver for computers without Bluetooth.

reviews.title: Check Your Computer First
reviews.text:
I sure hated to send this beauty back to Amazon, but I gave it a fair trial and I'm just not satisfied, and here's why.
It's a beautiful piece of machinery/artwork and weighs in at about 10 lbs. The keyboard glows in such a way that it will illuminate your work area in a dark room, and you can adjust the brightness up or down or off.
No, my complaint is with the speed at which it reacts. This keyboard has to keep up with you, possibly in order to mimic an old fashioned Royal or Underwood. I'm used to a $30 Logitech wireless that I can wag to the kitchen with my laptop. I don't like to use the laptop keyboard and scroll pad, I like a separate keyboard, it's faster for me. I find myself correcting errors constantly, and it slows me down, but I'm no stenographer either.
This Azio is Bluetooth, but not all computers support it. My 6 year old Toshiba doesn't, so I have to use the USB connection cord and block a precious port full time.
What a shame, it sure is elegant, and a lot of folks won't care about the perceived lag time.
The star rating posed a problem for me because I'm trying to be honest about my needs and I realize another might be perfectly satisfied.
This brute is expensive, and I'm not inclined to spend north of $700 for a new computer when I don't need one, just to support it.

reviews.title: Good keyboard with one minor catch
reviews.text: Very good keyboard, but it is heavier than I thought.  The height is not easier adjusted so I have to leave my drop down keyboard tray stuck out.

reviews.title: Great Keyboard - Bad Reliability Plus Non-Existent Support
reviews.text: Ordered this roughly a year ago. Started having problems with the mouse within a few months, with the mouse not holding a charge. Got the product replaced under warranty. Replacement mouse never would connect via the USB dongle consistently. 10 months in or so, my keyboard started acting up and, despite being plugged in via USB-C on my desk, continually shows out of power / red light. When I move it to Bluetooth it strangely works, so it can't be out of power. Reached out to Azio support and got crickets. Now have a nearly $200 set of pretty chrome and leather and plastic paperweight. Looks great, fabulous when it work. Sadly that's not very consistently or long. I've got cheap keyboards that lasted multiple years easily.

reviews.title: Not Bad
reviews.text: The keyboard and mouse was packed well for the shipping and arrived in great shape.  Very tactile feel and old school look.

reviews.title: Incorrect Description Information - Wrong item
reviews.text: First off it is very cute and I loved it, but the description says wireless/wired, giving the indication that it can be used wireless and it cannot.  This is a lot of money for a keyboard that has to be plugged in.  Also, I can’t tell how to get in touch with the seller so I can get confirmation.  RETURNING.

title: MELETRIX WS Grey Tactile Mechanical Keyboard Lubed Switches(35pcs)

features: Nylon Top and bottom housing, POM silent tactile stem
Double stage spring: 63.5g
Total travel: 3.50±0.3mm Pre-travel: 2.00±0.5mm Bottom-out force: 63.5
Factory lubed

reviews.title: Can be used stock!
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:528307786b36a4c2c9833ddd389101cb]] They come with a small amount of lube pre applied. Usually, prelubed switches are inconsistent in my experience but these ones are really good. I decided to not lube them at all. EDIT: got a few more batches, they're not as consistent. But seller was nice enough to get them replaced.

The stems are long pole. As a result, it has a nice bottom out sound.

It was noticeably heavier coming from a 50g switch to these that are 63g, but overtime I got adjusted and had no problems typing with them.

Overall a great buy, though I wish it was priced more competitively.

Using on Drop Preonic with MT3 Camillo, plateless, tape mod

reviews.title: Nice linears
reviews.text:
Starting off not sure who's at fault for this as it's only fulfilled by Amazon but one of my bags was missing a few switches (one bag was open and all the switches were rattling around inside the amazon pouch it arrived in) I counted 5 missing and there wasn't a silica gel packet either, the other bags were fine.
complaint aside they feel really nice and the factory lube seems to be well done as wish they had a "3 pin" option and different switch amounts 35 2 is too few and 3 is too many (unless some get lost in transit like mine lol) 90 would be a good option for the TKL/75% gang

reviews.title: Will be Using Right Away
reviews.text: In building & repairing computers these key switches are used very often. Mainly for high-end (over $100) keyboards that the serious gamers use. What they do is tough on the keyboards and often switches break or become less responsive which may lead to a gamer losing a match. These snap right in to the sockets and can instantly rebuild the whole keyboard like new. I realize they would not be used by your everyday person or casual gamer but when it gets serious they are  the switches to be used.

reviews.title: Nice Linear
reviews.text: These are nice and smooth and sound wonderful.  I'm not a linear fan usually, but I do like them in my modifier keys (shift, backspace, enter, and space) because I don't like the return sound a tactile makes while in a larger key.  These are perfect for that as they don't have a high pitch and there's no spring ping to even think of!  I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a lighter-weight linear switch with some style.

reviews.title: Great Stock!
reviews.text: The switches sounds great and feels good to use. The lube in this is not bad at all and you don't hear any ping or scratchiness. The bottom out is much more than I am used to, but I do enjoy it otherwise.

title: Metal Switch Opener GPL 205G0 0.35oz, Lubrication Oil Mechanical Keyboard Keycaps Aluminum Alloy Opener Instantly for Cherry mx Switches

It can turn on the switch in one second without any lag. We have tested and improved it several times. Compared with other switch openers, it has more personality, adds more surprising details, and is more smooth to use. This set includes a switch opener, as 4 Prong tool and a lubed brush. Compared to holding the switch by hand, I believe that the 4 Prong tool is a better choice for you. It can make your work process more professional and faster. The lubed Brush is slender and precise, and it can find exactly where you need lubrication. Product packaging:A Metal Switch Opener A 4 Stem Holder  A Lube Brush A GPL 205G0

reviews.title: Gamer kid loves this!
reviews.text: Bought as a gift for my gamer teen, and they love it! The kids are all into the clickity clack of keyboard sounds, and hers is so great now that her friends are asking her to clean and lube their keyboards and paying her to do it! Awesome product!

reviews.title: Almost everything you'll need to start fixing your keyboard.
reviews.text:
I was able to use this to make a 'cheap' Corsair board sound like a $300 board. The only other thing that would have made this a perfect kit is maybe a switch puller?

Definitely worth the price and the seller was quick to replace my missing items.

5 Feet long to reach your keyboard anywhere in your setup

reviews.title: Excellent quality cable and connectors, but color is more purple than blue
reviews.text:
Update: Oddly enough, this did not work with a Drop Alt keyboard. It worked with a few other keyboards just fine though. Just a word of warning for anyone pairing this cable with a Drop Alt (or maybe any other Drop keyboard).

This is the third coiled cable I have ordered, and it's definitely the best quality overall. The cable itself is thick and good quality. The coil is a bit firm, but nothing too unusual. The intermediate connector is all metal and heavy. Both sides of the intermediate connector can be unthreaded: The side going to the USB-C connector, and the side going to the USB connector.

One interesting thing I noticed was that the end connectors have a color matched almost shrink wrap thickness strain protection around them. The USB side is the most noticeable, and the USB-C side is a bit thicker. As long as there is enough strain protection, I actually prefer the less bulky end connections.

The only nit is the color. Even though it looked dark purplish in the product listing, I was hoping that it would be more blue in real life. It's not, it's an attractive dark purple. I would assume the color represented in the product listing is accurate, in case you're wondering if there is any color variation from the listing photos to real life.

title: VELOCIFIRE Keycap Puller Tool, 3 in 1 Keycap Puller, Stainless Steel Keycap Removal Tool, Key Pulling, Loosen, Tighten Keycap Stem, Expert for Mechanical Keyboard(Black)
features: This wire style keycap puller is a necessary tool for any mechanical keyboard owner. Easily remove and replace keycaps with this keycap removal tool.
Creative two-stage combination design, multifunctional keycap puller, one part is keycap puller, one part is keycap stem adjust.
You just need to place the keycap puller metal at the bottom of the keycap and pull the keycap out vertically.
Round body shape designed for maximum comfort.Compatible for most mechanical keyboards including Cherry MX，Kailh, Outemu, and Content Switches.
Wires can bend outward to fit onto every key except the spacebar. (The spacebar key can be removed by hand after the surrounding keys are removed).

reviews.title: It's good, but it doesn't fit Daskeyboard keys
reviews.text: I have a das keyboard.  This doesn't fit over the keys.  I had to twist and bend it to make it work.  It's not the right tool for a das keyboard.  With that said, it's a good quality tool.  I would buy it again for something with smaller keys.  Just don't bother for the mechanical das keyboard.

title: Hyekit Custom Gaming Keyboard - Alice Layout 66 Keys - Support QMK/Via Programmable Key - Gasket Structure - USB C Wired Mechanical Keyboard Kit - RGB Hot Swappable Switches & Keycaps - Acrylic Shell
description:
Note:
This link only sells a kit, not a finished keyboard, you need to assemble it yourself, the kit does not include switches, keycaps, data cables, or stabilizers.
About This Item
1.FancyAlice66 keyboard kit Alice is equipped with 66 keys, ergonomic, yes typing is more comfortable.  2. Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches.  3. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, kit supports QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions.  4. South-facing RGB Backlit has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight makes the keyboard look more attractive.  5.Gasket Mount, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, which makes the keyboard more comfortable and soft to type, and the typing sound is more pleasant.  6. Acrylic stacking shell, the texture is very good, enjoy the fun of custom assembling the shell.  7. Wired mode, support USB-C, typing response is faster, Type-c interface adapts to more data lines.  8. The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. First, ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly.  9.Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers.
Package includes:
Keyboard case*1  PCB*1  Quiet sandwich cotton*1  Switch pad*1  Assemble some parts
features: 【Package Included】Keyboard case*1, PCB*1, Quiet sandwich cotton*1, Switch pad*1, Assemble some parts. No USB cables, switches, stabilizer, and keycaps. (Note, The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. you ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly. Let's enjoy the process of assembling the keyboard and the great sense of achievement brought by the successful assembly.)

【Custom Gaming Keyboard】Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches. Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions. Wired mode, supports USB-C, typing response is faster.

【Alice Layout】The Alice layout considers the natural input angle of the wrist from the perspective of ergonomics. When using the conventional arrangement keyboard for continuous high-intensity input work, the unnatural bending of the wrist will easily lead to hand fatigue, and the Alice layout can solve this problem very well, it takes into account the integrity of the keyboard and the relatively relaxed input state of the wrist.

【South-facing RGB Backlit】Alice66 has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight, the RBG Backlight effect is very bright even in the daytime. The lower lamp position is designed so that the light will not be blocked by the switch. The fully transparent bottom cover allows the 16 bottom lights to display their backlight effects without obstruction, making the keyboard look more attractive.

【Acrylic Gasket Structure】Alice66 is designed with Gasket structure, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, the sound is pure, and the feel is soft. This wonderful feel can make the typing process very pleasant and full of fun.

reviews.title: Sounds great with no mods
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:58cc2520e806ff1428542a1df7456920]] Assembly was pretty easy for this DIY kit. It even sounds great without any modifications. I decided to put poron switch pads on the pcb since the foam it came with did not line up properly. The feet that comes with this kit is too aggressive for my liking. I decided to remove it and put clear rubber bumpons

Did not come with stabilizers. I was hoping it does at this price point. Speaking of which, this kit can be had for cheaper on other retailers. But ordering from Amazon gave me more confidence.

My copy came with cracked and scuffed case. I messaged the seller directly and they were able to help and send me a new case promptly. They also helped with VIA setup on this keyboard.

Pros:
- easy assembly
- VIA support PCB
- great sound with little to no modifications
- south facing, per key RGB
- under glow lighting
- A+ great customer support from seller

Cons:
- price seems higher compared to other websites
- inconsistent craftsmanship
- no stabilizers included
- front of the keyboard can be wobbly with the aggressive feet riser installed

(Keycaps: MT3 2048; Switches: WS Aurora Clears)

title: SteelSeries Apex Pro Mini Mechanical Gaming Keyboard – World’s Fastest Keyboard – Adjustable Actuation – Compact 60% Form Factor – RGB – PBT Keycaps – USB-C
description: The Apex Pro Mini Wireless brings the world's fastest keystrokes with OmniPoint 2.0, total adjustability of activation points, dual actuation, and a slim-form factor.
features: World's Fastest Keyboard - Overtake standard mechanical keyboards as OmniPoint 2.0 delivers 11x faster response and 10x swifter actuation.
Customize Every Keystroke - Precisely adjust the registration depth of every key to the nearest 0.1MM, going from speedy 0.2MM touches to deliberate 3.8MM presses.
2-in-1 Action Keys - Program two different actions for the same key, such as walking with a light touch and sprinting with a deeper press.
60% Form Factor - Streamlined design saves desk space and leaves more room for mouse movement, retaining full-size keyboard capabilities with side-printed functions.

Durable PBT Keycaps - Long-lasting double shot PBT keycaps provide a high-quality texture for enhanced keystroke feel and improved typing accuracy. Compatibility-OS-Windows, Xbox, PlayStation, and Mac OS X. USB port required(*Not all software features supported on Mac OS). Software-SteelSeries Engine for Windows (8.1 or newer) and Mac OSX (10.13 or newer) (*Not all software features supported on Mac OS)

reviews.title: Almost perfect, if not for the competition
reviews.text:
TLDR: If you don't want to deal with pre-ordering the Wooting60HE or just really like Steelseries products, this is a really good 60% option.

Pros:
-Omnipoint switches feel very good
-Adjustable actuation point
-Finally adopted PBT keycaps for longevity
-Good RGB brightness and accuracy
-Pretty good stabs out of the box made better with some lube

Cons:
-The Wooting60HE exists...

Thoughts:
Rant time. Right out of the gate, this would be the perfect 60% keyboard currently, if the Wooting60HE didn't exist. It has hotswap switches, acoustic foam, analog functionality, adjustable actuation point, RAPID TRIGGER (huge for high actuation point users), pre-lubed switches and stabs, and doesn't require its software to be active to function fully. This board isn't bad by any means, it's just that the wooting is just better in my opinion. I saw steelseries' video today comparing their "response time" with some weird rigged comparison test where they claim a 10ms advantage over the wooting. Even if that were true, which I don't think is accurate, 10ms isn't enough to justify choosing this over the wooting, unless you just really like Steelseries products, which is fine. But the rapid trigger functionality alone is HUGE for switches with a high actuation. If you don't know what that is, basically at any point during the upward travel (after you've pressed a key), you can press down again, to register another keypress. It basically makes the actuation dynamic letting you keep that high actuation, but not require you to fully release the key to press it again. Anyway, rant/PSA over, back to the Apex.

Despite everything I just said, this is still a really good keyboard. If you liked the Apex Pro/TKL, you'll like this. I've found that magnetic switches are easily the smoothest ones on the market, even without lubing. They just glide, absolutely fantastic.

I would comment on the responsiveness, but honestly, I really can't imagine milliseconds making a huge difference for movement inputs in a shooter. Osu probably if that's your thing, but starting to move in one direction or another a milisecond faster isn't going to change your game in the slightest. A mouse, absolutely, but there's really no keyboard input that needs that level of precision. So try not to get hung up on responsiveness in regards to keyboard claims.

Something definitely worth noting is how magnetic switches and optical switches are basically immune to double presses that eventually happen to regular mechanical switches. You can rest assure that all of your key presses will be very accurate.

Conclusion:
Before you commit to this, I highly recommend looking into the Wooting60HE. If you don't want to deal with pre-ordering, or just really like Steelseries products, this is still a really good keyboard. I love the switches, and am so happy they decided to finally start using PBT keycaps.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: I don’t know why people are hating on this keyboard so much? unless you’re like a nerd and actually care about the .2mm and stuff about the price. The keys so really nice and smooth and is a very quiet keyboard, if you want to drop this much for a keyboard then go for it i think it was a good purchase for me

reviews.title: Perfect for Gaming
reviews.text: 15 year old gamer approved! It allows for quicker response times in games, lights are bright and stay on. The keys are a little noisy but if you’re into ASMR, it’s relaxing!

reviews.title: Takes some getting used to.
reviews.text: This mini keyboard was a major change from my previous keyboard. The first major shock I had when I needed the grave/tilde key for programming, thankfully with the SteelSeries GG program I was able to program the grave/tilde key into the escape key with a modifier. In Minecraft as well some of the F3 functions needed to be mapped because there is no function row. After some getting used to I am just as fast on this keyboard as my previous one. There was a quirk I could not figure out without some googling however, if you hold the SteelSeries logo and the windows button it locks the windows button and changes it's color to white. Doing it again will re-enable the windows button.
